summary: Deterministic proof for the review-variants read-mostly boundary: the public CLI passes the candidate workspace as an inspectable placeholder, writes variant artifacts to the run output directory, and a review-only variant leaves the candidate git workspace unchanged.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: evaluate review variants read-mostly candidate boundary
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the product-owned review-variants artifact placement and review-only candidate-inspection behavior are marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Cautilus can prevent an arbitrary consumer-owned shell command from mutating `{candidate_repo}`.
Every external reviewer CLI is sandboxed read-only by default.
Every consumer adapter uses the recommended Codex `--sandbox read-only` guardrail.
Review variants are appropriate for intentional mutation or improvement loops.

protectedBehaviors: `evaluate review variants` passes `{candidate_repo}` as the explicit workspace under review instead of relying on host-local paths.
A review-only variant can inspect the candidate workspace and produce structured verdict JSON without changing `git status --short` in that candidate workspace.
Variant artifacts are written under the review output directory, not inside the candidate workspace.
Missing output files, output-under-test references, and prompt-input schema defaults remain covered by focused CLI smoke tests.

decision.reason: The focused CLI smoke tests prove the product-owned review-variants path treats `{candidate_repo}` as an explicit workspace under review, writes variant outputs to the review output directory, and leaves a clean candidate git workspace unchanged for a review-only variant. The bundle explicitly excludes arbitrary consumer-owned command mutation prevention.
